examples: a deliberate attempt to cover up his crime

definitions.text: Formed with deliberation; carefully considered; not sudden or rash.
examples: a deliberate opinion; a deliberate measure or result

definitions.text: Of a person, weighing facts and arguments with a view to a choice or decision; carefully considering the probable consequences of a step; slow in determining.
examples: The jury took eight hours to come to its deliberate verdict.

definitions.text: To consider carefully; to weigh well in the mind.
examples: It is now time for the jury to deliberate the guilt of the defendant.

definitions.text: To consider the reasons for and against anything; to reflect.
